question: Based on the stacked area chart showing quarterly restaurant performance indicators, which statement best describes what we can conclude about overall quarterly performance?

options.A: Q2 has the best overall performance because it reaches the highest combined total of all indicators.
options.B: Q4 has the best overall performance because it maintains moderate values for all indicators.
options.C: Q1 has the best overall performance because it has the lowest combined total of all indicators.
options.D: The chart cannot determine overall performance accurately because it combines restaurant metrics with different units into one total.

explanation: The chart stacks unrelated metrics (Sales, Kitchen Temperature, etc.) into a cumulative area. These metrics have different units and meanings, so adding them together creates a meaningless total. The stacked area cannot be used to assess overall performance. Only individual metric analysis is valid.
